title: rhamnus smithii subsp. fasciculata
intro: Rhamnus smithii subsp. fasciculata, also known as the fragrant buckthorn, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae, native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever and inflammation, and it is also used to treat various ailments such as diarrhea, dysentery, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
